title: VerdictEvent
description: The message emitted by the execution side across the Build seam (spec §5.1), to an event stream, when a unit reaches a verdict. SELF-DESCRIBING: a consumer that never observed the emission can reconcile it from the event alone. The executor emits and is done — it does not wait, retry, or know who listens (the producer holds no knowledge of any consumer). The universal envelope below is what every conforming executor must emit; stack-specific evidence (per-cell results, tier actually run, etc.) lives in `executor_extension`, which a consumer may ignore.

event_id.description: Unique identifier for this emitted event.

emitted_at.description: When the executor emitted the verdict (RFC 3339 / ISO 8601). A verdict emitted while no consumer is listening sits in the stream until one next reads it — temporal decoupling — so the timestamp is the emit time, not a consume time.

unit_ref.description: Echoes the WorkUnit's `unit_ref`, letting a consumer match the verdict to the unit with no shared state.

parent_lineage.description: Echoes the WorkUnit's `parent_lineage` (if it carried one), enabling a consumer to roll the verdict up to its deliverable. Omitted if the unit carried none.

bundle_hash.description: The exact frozen bundle this verdict ran against — equal to the WorkUnit's `bundle_hash`. Closes the loop: every verdict is attributable to an immutable input, so provenance is reproducible (spec §5.1, §6 success criteria).

verdict.description: The verdict, drawn from the Execution Contract / framework verdict vocabulary (spec §6.2) — a declared artifact, never a boolean in an exit code. `accepted`: the artifact met its schema and gates. `rejected`: it did not. `escalate`: the executor could not reach an accept/reject under its current capability and recommends a consumer re-dispatch at greater capability.

next_consequence.description: The Transition Contract consequence the executor applied or recommends (spec §6.2, §7). Optional: a consumer may act on `verdict` alone. When present, it binds verdict to its declared next step — e.g. `escalate` signals that re-dispatch at greater capability is the recommended move, which the consumer (not the executor) decides to perform.

executor_extension.description: THE ONE OPAQUE SLOT. Stack-specific evidence a particular execution framework produces — per-cell or per-step results from walking a sealed interior, the capability/tier actually run, heterogeneity metrics, timing, and so on — lives here. A consumer MAY ignore it entirely and act on `verdict` alone; it is present for audit and for executors that want to surface their interior. The framework neither reads nor mandates its contents. A `kind` discriminator is recommended.

kind.description: Optional discriminator naming the execution framework whose evidence this slot carries; should match the WorkUnit's `executor_extension.kind`.
